summary: Bug fix verified: All three 'Download CV' download triggers now work end-to-end. Hero, Navbar, and Contact section CV links are valid <a> elements with correct href '/cv-srikan-dewasumithra.pdf' and download attribute. Playwright's page.expect_download captured a real browser download event for each. The public PDF asset is reachable at REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL/cv-srikan-dewasumithra.pdf returning HTTP 200, content-type application/pdf, content-length 138813 (non-empty).
critical_code_review_comments: Minor: Contact section's resume anchor uses empty 'download' attribute (download=""), so the browser falls back to the URL filename (cv-srikan-dewasumithra.pdf) instead of the friendly 'Srikan-Dewasumithra-CV.pdf' used by Hero and Navbar. Consider aligning by setting download="Srikan-Dewasumithra-CV.pdf" in /app/frontend/src/components/sections/Contact.jsx for consistent download filename UX.
Three identical CV anchor blocks could be extracted into a reusable <CvDownloadLink /> component to keep filename and href DRY.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Bug fix for 'Download CV' button is fully verified. Hero (data-testid=hero-download-cv), Navbar (data-testid=nav-download-cv), and Contact section anchor are all rendered as <A> tags pointing to /cv-srikan-dewasumithra.pdf with the download attribute. Playwright expect_download fired in all three cases. PDF served at the public URL with correct content-type application/pdf and 138813 bytes. Only nit: Contact anchor has empty download attribute so suggested filename differs (cv-srikan-dewasumithra.pdf vs Srikan-Dewasumithra-CV.pdf).
rca of the issue: Original bug: A <Button> (which renders an HTML <button>) was nested inside an <a> tag. Invalid HTML — browsers strip the nesting and the click did not navigate/trigger the anchor's download. Fix uses Radix Slot via shadcn Button asChild so the <a> element itself receives the Button styling. Verified by inspecting el.tagName === 'A' for both hero-download-cv and nav-download-cv, and by capturing real Playwright download events on click.
